proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 16 binary features, at least one binary feature has a non-zero univariable association with objective_response (i.e., the response rate differs between feature=1 and feature=0).
Among the 19 continuous features, at least one continuous feature differs in mean value between responders (objective_response=1) and non-responders (objective_response=0).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2,p,_,_ = stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Chi-square univariable screen across 16 binary features. Five features show p<1e-4: feature_014 (resp 0.235 vs 0.167, OR 1.53, p=5.8e-70), feature_001 (0.221 vs 0.165, OR 1.43, p=1.9e-55), feature_021 (0.220 vs 0.166, OR 1.41, p=6.6e-51), feature_026 (0.168 vs 0.191, OR 0.86, p=3.3e-6), and feature_009 (0.166 vs 0.190, OR 0.85, p=3.5e-5). The remaining 11 binary features show no significant association (all p > 0.1). Strongest signal is feature_014.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: t,p = stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,c], df.loc[y==0,c]); auc = mannwhitney_auc(df[c], y)
analyses.result_summary: Two-sample t-tests across 19 continuous features. feature_004 (range 0-2, ECOG-like) is the strongest discriminator: mean 0.669 in responders vs 0.826 in non-responders, AUC=0.439, p=1.0e-91 — i.e., higher feature_004 strongly predicts non-response. Other significant directions: feature_024 (m_resp 58.5 vs 60.3, AUC 0.472, p=2.3e-16, lower in responders), feature_020 (3.44 vs 3.95, AUC 0.465, p=8.0e-31, lower in responders), feature_029 (3.82 vs 3.79, AUC 0.517, p=2.8e-7, higher in responders), feature_027 (5.69 vs 6.17, p=6.0e-6, lower in responders), feature_022 (19.3 vs 20.4, p=1.8e-4, lower in responders).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutually adjusting all 35 features in a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response, at least three binary features (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) remain independently positively associated with response, while feature_026 and feature_009 remain independently negatively associated with response.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_004 (likely ECOG performance status) remains the single strongest continuous predictor of non-response, with response decreasing as feature_004 increases.

analyses.code: Newton-Raphson logistic regression of objective_response on all 35 features (continuous standardized).
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit shows feature_014 (beta=+0.436, OR=1.55 per allele, z=+17.9, p<1e-300), feature_001 (beta=+0.374, OR=1.45, z=+15.96, p<1e-300), feature_021 (beta=+0.362, OR=1.44, z=+15.4, p<1e-300) as independent positive predictors; feature_009 (beta=-0.188, OR=0.83, z=-4.64, p=3.6e-6) and feature_026 (beta=-0.169, OR=0.85, z=-4.99, p=6.1e-7) as negative. feature_004 is the strongest continuous: beta=-0.245 per SD, OR=0.78 per SD, z=-20.4, p<1e-90. Other significant continuous: feature_020 (beta=-0.142/SD, p<1e-30), feature_024 (beta=-0.100/SD, p<1e-16), feature_029 (beta=+0.061/SD, p=1.4e-7), feature_027 (beta=-0.060/SD, p=5.8e-6), feature_022 (beta=-0.048/SD, p=1.3e-4). All other features non-significant at p<0.05 after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three positively-associated binary features feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are pairwise statistically independent of each other (no co-occurrence enrichment), suggesting they capture distinct underlying constructs (e.g., one regimen plus two biomarkers).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_001'], df['feature_014']) etc.
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise correlation phi-coefficients among feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 are all |phi|<0.005 (p>0.4 for each). Observed co-occurrence counts match independence to within ~30 per cell. The three features behave as statistically independent indicators in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 and feature_014 interact positively on objective_response: the response benefit of feature_001=1 (vs 0) is larger when feature_014=1 than when feature_014=0.
feature_001 and feature_021 interact positively on objective_response: the response benefit of feature_001=1 (vs 0) is larger when feature_021=1 than when feature_021=0.
feature_014 and feature_021 interact positively on objective_response.

analyses.code: logreg(y ~ feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_001:feature_014)
analyses.result_summary: Two-way logit with interaction: beta(f001*f014) = +1.060, OR_int=2.89, z=+21.5, p<1e-100. Main effects of f001 and f014 are essentially zero in this model (beta ~ -0.01, -0.07), so virtually all of their joint effect arises from the interaction.

analyses.code: logreg(y ~ feature_001 + feature_021 + feature_001:feature_021)
analyses.result_summary: Two-way logit: beta(f001*f021) = +0.883, OR_int=2.42, z=+18.8, p<1e-78. Again, main effects of f001 and f021 collapse to near zero in this model.

analyses.code: logreg(y ~ feature_014 + feature_021 + feature_014:feature_021)
analyses.result_summary: Two-way logit: beta(f014*f021) = +1.055, OR_int=2.87, z=+21.4, p<1e-100. Main effects collapse to near zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response to objective_response is governed by a three-way interaction f001 x f014 x f021: the response rate is elevated only in the joint subgroup where all three features = 1, and the lower-order (pairwise and main) terms add little once the three-way term is included.

analyses.code: logreg(y ~ f001 + f014 + f021 + all pairwise + f001*f014*f021)
analyses.result_summary: In a saturated three-way model, the three-way coefficient beta(f001*f014*f021) = +2.126, OR=8.38, z=+20.4, p<1e-90. None of the three main effects nor any of the three pairwise interactions are significant at p<0.05 in the same model (largest is f001*f021 at p=0.085). Almost the entire response signal is concentrated in the cell where all three indicators are simultaneously 1.

analyses.code: df.groupby(['feature_001','feature_014','feature_021'])['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Empirical 8-cell tabulation: response rate is 0.633 in the (f001=1,f014=1,f021=1) cell (n=2401) vs 0.157-0.172 in each of the other 7 cells (each n>=2400). The response excess is therefore +0.46 absolute, concentrated in the single triple-positive cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 (presumed adverse marker) suppresses the response benefit of the (f001=1,f014=1,f021=1) triple-positive subgroup: within that subgroup, response rate drops sharply when feature_026=1 vs feature_026=0.
feature_009 (second presumed adverse marker) similarly suppresses the response benefit of the (f001=1,f014=1,f021=1) triple-positive subgroup.

analyses.code: Within (f001=1 & f014=1 & f021=1), compare response by feature_026.
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple-positive (1,1,1) subgroup (n=2401, baseline response 0.633): feature_026=0 (n=2025) shows response 0.717 vs feature_026=1 (n=376) shows response 0.184 (difference -0.533, OR-like ratio (0.717/0.283)/(0.184/0.816)=11.2). Logit interaction HOT*feature_026 in joint model: beta=-2.66, OR=0.07, z=-17.7, p<1e-50. feature_026 essentially abolishes the triple-positive effect.

analyses.code: Within (f001=1 & f014=1 & f021=1), compare response by feature_009.
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple-positive (1,1,1) subgroup: feature_009=0 (n=2125) shows response 0.691 vs feature_009=1 (n=276) shows response 0.188 (difference -0.503). Logit interaction HOT*feature_009: beta=-2.55, OR=0.078, z=-14.9, p<1e-40. feature_009 also essentially abolishes the triple-positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The cleanest 5-feature definition of the responder subgroup is: feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0. In this subgroup, the response rate is at least 0.7, vs near baseline (~0.17) in patients failing any single one of these conditions.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup defined by (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1, f026=0, f009=0): n=1790 (3.6% of cohort), response rate 0.786. Complement: n=48,210, response rate 0.165. Risk difference +0.621; odds ratio 18.55. Chi-square p effectively 0. This 5-feature predicate captures essentially all of the structured response signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_001 as the candidate treatment, the heterogeneity of the feature_001 effect on objective_response is dominated by feature_014 and feature_021 (positive modifiers) and feature_009 and feature_026 (negative modifiers). No other binary feature significantly modifies the feature_001 effect after Bonferroni-style consideration of the 15 tests.

analyses.code: for F in binary_cols\{f001}: logreg(y ~ f001 + F + f001:F)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 x binary-feature interaction screen ranked by p-value: f014 (beta=+1.060, p<1e-100), f021 (beta=+0.883, p<1e-78), f026 (beta=-0.375, p=3.3e-8), f009 (beta=-0.281, p=5.0e-4). Other interactions: f032 (p=0.029, beta=+0.187), f008 (p=0.037), f006 (p=0.066) — none survive Bonferroni correction over 15 tests (alpha=0.0033). Heterogeneity of feature_001's effect is thus dominated by feature_014 and feature_021.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_014 as the candidate treatment, the heterogeneity of its effect on objective_response is again dominated by feature_001, feature_021 (positive modifiers) and feature_026, feature_009 (negative modifiers).
Treating feature_021 as the candidate treatment, the heterogeneity of its effect on objective_response is dominated by feature_001, feature_014 (positive) and feature_009, feature_026 (negative).

analyses.code: for F in binary_cols\{f014}: logreg(y ~ f014 + F + f014:F)
analyses.result_summary: feature_014 x binary-feature interaction screen: f001 (beta=+1.060, p<1e-100), f021 (beta=+1.055, p<1e-100), f026 (beta=-0.604, p=2.2e-16), f009 (beta=-0.468, p=6.8e-8). All others non-significant after correction. Confirms the same trio of positive modifiers and same two adverse markers.

analyses.code: for F in binary_cols\{f021}: logreg(y ~ f021 + F + f021:F)
analyses.result_summary: feature_021 x binary-feature interaction screen: f014 (beta=+1.055, p<1e-100), f001 (beta=+0.883, p<1e-78), f009 (beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-7), f026 (beta=-0.306, p=6.0e-6). Others non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous features do NOT meaningfully modify the feature_001 effect: in a series of feature_001 x continuous-feature interaction tests, no continuous feature shows an interaction p < 0.001.

analyses.code: for F in cont_cols: standardize F; logreg(y ~ f001 + Fz + f001:Fz)
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 x continuous-feature interaction screen across 19 features: only feature_020 reaches p<0.01 (beta=+0.075/SD, z=+3.09, p=2.0e-3); next strongest is feature_024 (p=0.074). 17 of 19 features have interaction p>0.10. Continuous features therefore do not materially modify the feature_001 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder-enriched subgroup (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1, f026=0, f009=0), feature_004 (likely ECOG performance status, 0/1/2) negatively predicts response in a graded fashion: response rate decreases as feature_004 increases from 0 to 1 to 2.
Within the same responder-enriched subgroup, feature_020 (peripheral-blast-percentage-like) is lower in responders than non-responders.
No binary feature outside the (f001, f014, f021, f026, f009) set is associated with response within the responder-enriched subgroup.

analyses.code: Within HOT subgroup, df.groupby('feature_004')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within HOT subgroup (n=1790): f004=0 (n=617) response 0.859; f004=1 (n=902) response 0.772; f004=2 (n=271) response 0.668. Two-sample t-test of feature_004 between responders and non-responders within HOT: m_resp=0.752 vs m_nonr=1.008, diff=-0.256, p=4.4e-11. Strong graded inverse association.

analyses.code: Within HOT subgroup, t-test feature_020 by response.
analyses.result_summary: Within HOT subgroup, feature_020 mean is 3.77 in responders vs 4.56 in non-responders, diff -0.79, p=4.0e-4. Confirms that even among the otherwise favorable subgroup, higher peripheral-blast-like burden (feature_020) reduces response probability.

analyses.code: Within HOT subgroup, chi-square for each remaining binary feature.
analyses.result_summary: Within HOT subgroup, no binary feature outside (f001, f014, f021, f026, f009) reaches p<0.05 in chi-square: best are feature_032 (p=0.118), feature_015 (p=0.124), feature_019 (p=0.132). Confirms that the predictive structure is captured by the five-feature subgroup definition plus feature_004 (ECOG-like) and feature_020 (blast-burden-like) for fine-grained risk.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 has a negative main effect on objective_response that is largely driven by its suppressor effect within the (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1) triple-positive subgroup: outside that subgroup, feature_026 shows little to no association with response.
feature_009 likewise shows its negative association with response only within the (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1) triple-positive subgroup, not outside it.

analyses.code: Stratify by triple-positive vs not, compare response by feature_026.
analyses.result_summary: Outside the (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1) cell (n=47,599): response rate is 0.166 with feature_026=1 vs 0.166 with feature_026=0 (diff effectively 0; chi-square p=0.86). Inside the (1,1,1) cell: 0.184 (f026=1) vs 0.717 (f026=0). The negative main effect of feature_026 in the full cohort is therefore essentially a pure interaction with the triple-positive subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratify by triple-positive vs not, compare response by feature_009.
analyses.result_summary: Outside the (1,1,1) cell, feature_009=1 vs 0 response: 0.166 vs 0.166 (no signal). Inside the (1,1,1) cell: 0.188 (f009=1) vs 0.691 (f009=0). Same pattern: feature_009's negative main effect is a pure interaction with the triple-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining 'treatment' as feature_001 and the joint biomarker subgroup as (feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0), the absolute treatment effect of feature_001 on objective_response is enormous within the biomarker-positive subgroup and negligible outside it.

analyses.code: Within biomarker-positive subgroup (f014=1 & f021=1 & f026=0 & f009=0), compare response by feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: Biomarker-positive subgroup (f014=1 & f021=1 & f026=0 & f009=0): with feature_001=1 (n=1790) response 0.786; with feature_001=0 (n=2789) response 0.158. Treatment effect of feature_001: +0.628 absolute, OR ~21. Outside the biomarker-positive subgroup (n=45,421), response 0.182 (f001=1) vs 0.171 (f001=0), diff +0.011 (chi-square p=0.013, trivial magnitude). The feature_001 effect is essentially absent outside the biomarker-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining 'treatment' alternatively as feature_014, the same pattern holds: treatment benefit is concentrated in the (feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0) subgroup.
Defining 'treatment' alternatively as feature_021, the same pattern holds: treatment benefit is concentrated in the (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0) subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified comparison of feature_014 effect within (f001=1 & f021=1 & f026=0 & f009=0).
analyses.result_summary: Within (f001=1 & f021=1 & f026=0 & f009=0) (n=4084): f014=1 (n=1790) response 0.786 vs f014=0 (n=2294) response 0.156. Effect +0.630 absolute, OR ~22. Outside this subgroup the f014 effect is tiny (response 0.165 vs 0.169). Symmetric to the feature_001 finding.

analyses.code: Stratified comparison of feature_021 effect within (f001=1 & f014=1 & f026=0 & f009=0).
analyses.result_summary: Within (f001=1 & f014=1 & f026=0 & f009=0): f021=1 (n=1790) response 0.786 vs f021=0 (n=2293) response 0.158. Effect +0.628 absolute, OR ~22. Outside subgroup the f021 effect is again tiny. Confirms the symmetric three-way structure: any of f001, f014, f021 looks like a 'treatment' that only works when the other two are present and neither adverse marker is present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ECOG-like feature feature_004 modifies response within the responder-enriched subgroup but does NOT meaningfully modify the binary treatment-effect terms (feature_001 effect): once the five-feature subgroup is defined, feature_004 acts approximately as an additional risk factor rather than as a treatment-effect modifier.

analyses.code: logreg(y ~ HOT + suppressors + f004_z + HOT:f004_z).
analyses.result_summary: Joint logit with HOT indicator, f026, f009, HOT*f026, HOT*f009, f004_z, and HOT*f004_z: feature_004_z main effect beta=-0.261/SD, OR=0.77 per SD, z=-20.3, p<1e-90. HOT*feature_004_z interaction: beta=-0.060, z=-1.15, p=0.25. feature_004 thus exerts a strong, generally-applied negative effect on response but is not a significant modifier of the treatment-by-biomarker subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The two adverse-marker-like features feature_026 and feature_009 are not synergistic with each other beyond their independent suppressor effects: HOT*feature_026*feature_009 three-way interaction is not significant.

analyses.code: Within HOT, response by (f026, f009).
analyses.result_summary: Within (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1) subgroup: (f026=0, f009=0) n=1790 resp=0.786; (f026=0, f009=1) n=235 resp=0.187; (f026=1, f009=0) n=335 resp=0.182; (f026=1, f009=1) n=41 resp=0.195. Either suppressor alone collapses response to the baseline rate; having both does not push response below baseline. There is therefore no meaningful synergy between the two suppressors — each is sufficient on its own to abolish the favorable subgroup benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No additional binary feature, beyond {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021, feature_026, feature_009}, contributes a 'sufficient' or 'necessary' condition for response: enumerating all 1- and 2-feature changes to the favorable subgroup definition does not yield a higher response rate.

analyses.code: For each pair of remaining binary features (f), test whether further restriction (f=0 or f=1) within HOT raises response rate above 0.786.
analyses.result_summary: Within HOT, all remaining 11 binary features show no significant association with response (all p>=0.12). The maximum within-HOT response rate from any binary refinement is ~0.84 (feature_032=1 within HOT, n=160), but its chi-square p=0.12 — within sampling variation. No additional binary predicate is needed to characterize the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported final treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis: Among patients in this AML cohort, the binary treatment indicator feature_001 increases the probability of objective_response only within the biomarker-positive subgroup defined by {feature_014=1, feature_021=1, feature_026=0, feature_009=0}; in this subgroup the response rate rises to ~0.79 vs ~0.16 baseline (absolute treatment effect ~+0.63), whereas outside this subgroup feature_001 has at most a ~+0.01 absolute effect on response. The same conclusion holds symmetrically when feature_014 or feature_021 is treated as the 'treatment' indicator and the other two of {feature_001, feature_014, feature_021} together with the absence of {feature_026, feature_009} are treated as the biomarker subgroup.

analyses.code: Final 5-feature subgroup test: response rate in (f001=1 & f014=1 & f021=1 & f026=0 & f009=0) vs all others.
analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup test confirms: n=1790 in favorable subgroup with response 0.786; n=48,210 elsewhere with response 0.165; risk difference +0.621; OR 18.55; chi-square p effectively 0. Subgroup defines essentially all of the structured response signal in the cohort.
